senses.definition: almost impossible to predict who will win and who will lose, particularly in a competition

examples.x: The race was too close to call until the final seconds when one team scored a winning goal.
In the election, the two leading candidates were neck and neck in the polls, making it too close to call a clear winner.
The talent competition was so fierce that it was too close to call, and the judges had a tough decision to make.
With the scores tied in the championship game, it was too close to call who would emerge as the victor.
The film awards ceremony saw two outstanding performances, and it was too close to call which actor would win the Best Actor category.

word_list.pronunciation: ɐ kwˈɛstʃən mˈɑːɹk hˈæŋ ˌoʊvɚ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: a feeling of uncertainty and doubt about something

examples.x: A question mark hangs over the future of the company and I'm sure you don't need me to remind you of that.
A question mark hangs over the company's financial stability due to recent losses.
The mysterious disappearance of the artifact left a question mark hanging over the museum's security measures.
The sudden change in weather conditions has left a question mark hanging over the planned outdoor event.

senses.definition: to clear something of any misunderstanding or misinterpretation

examples.x: In his press conference, the spokesperson aimed to set the record straight regarding the company's recent financial performance.
After the rumors spread about their relationship, they decided to put the record straight by publicly confirming their engagement.
The documentary was produced to put the record straight about the historical events and dispel common myths.
She felt it was essential to set the record straight about her qualifications and experience during the job interview.
The athlete used a public statement to put the record straight about the doping allegations, asserting their innocence.

senses.definition: used to say that even if the happening of something is unlikely, it is not impossible

examples.x: There might be an even bigger one–I doubt it, but you never know.
You never know, she might change her mind.
You never know, your dream job might be just around the corner.
I'll apply for the scholarship; you never know if I might get it.
Sometimes, you never know who you'll meet and what opportunities will come your way.

senses.definition: a situation that is hard to define or categorize and therefore unclear

examples.x: When it comes to environmental regulations, there's a gray area where economic interests clash with ecological concerns.
In artistic interpretation, there's a creative gray area that allows for diverse perspectives and meanings.
The ethics of using animals in medical testing can be a gray area, as it involves balancing scientific progress with animal welfare.
The boundaries of privacy in the digital age often exist in a gray area, raising important questions about personal data and surveillance.

senses.definition: to start doubting a decision and begin to wonder whether it is the right or best thing to do

examples.x: It seems like our client has second thoughts.
After accepting the job offer, he had second thoughts about relocating to a new city and leaving behind his friends.
She was planning to buy a new car but had second thoughts when she saw the steep monthly payments.
He had second thoughts about proposing after learning more about her financial irresponsibility.
The team was about to implement a new strategy, but they had second thoughts when they considered the potential risks.

senses.definition: an attempt to guess or do something without having sufficient information or knowing the consequences

examples.x: I had no idea how to fix the leaky faucet, so I made a stab in the dark and tried adjusting the valve, and it worked.
When it came to choosing a career path, she made a stab in the dark and ended up finding her true passion.
His guess about the password was a stab in the dark, but miraculously, he got it right.
The detective took a stab in the dark by questioning the witness, hoping for a breakthrough in the case.
Investing in stocks can sometimes feel like a stab in the dark, as market conditions can be unpredictable.

senses.definition: used to refer to something that is in an uncertain state

examples.x: The election results are in the balance, and it's too close to call a winner at this point.
The patient's recovery is still in the balance, and the doctors are closely monitoring their condition.
With only seconds left on the clock, the game was in the balance, and the next goal would decide the winner.
The success of the new product launch is in the balance, pending customer feedback and sales figures.

word_list.pronunciation: biː ˈɛnɪwˌʌnz ɔːɹ ˈɛnɪbˌɑːdiz ɡˈɛs ɔːɹ kˈɔːl

senses.definition: to be difficult or almost impossible to predict the outcome of a situation or event

examples.x: When it will stop raining is anyone's guess; the weather has been so unpredictable.
The number of attendees at the event is anyone's guess; we'll just have to wait and see.
The outcome of the negotiations is anyone's guess at this point, as both parties remain tight-lipped.
After the unexpected resignation of the CEO, the future of the company was anybody's guess.
With the current political climate, the election's outcome in two years will be anybody's guess.
The outcome of the match was so close that it could be anyone's call as to who would win.
With both candidates receiving equal support, the election result seemed to be anyone's call.
Given the unpredictable nature of the stock market, it's anyone's call whether the company's shares will rise or fall tomorrow.

word_list.pronunciation: tˈeɪk ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ wɪð ɐ ɡɹˈeɪn ʌv sˈɑːlt

senses.definition: to only accept a part of something or not take it seriously at all

examples.x: When reading online reviews, it's essential to take them with a grain of salt, as some may not be genuine.
He tends to exaggerate his accomplishments, so I always take his claims with a grain of salt.
When a rumor spreads, it's a good idea to take it with a grain of salt until you can verify the information.

senses.definition: a person or thing with uncertain or uncontrollable qualities or capabilities

examples.x: In the playoffs, the underdog team became the wild card, surprising everyone with their unexpected victories.
Her creative approach to problem-solving was like a wild card in the team, often leading to innovative solutions.
The sudden availability of additional funding was a wild card that allowed the project to expand beyond its initial scope.
When negotiating, it's crucial to consider the potential wild cards that could impact the outcome of the deal.

senses.definition: a thing that is nowhere as good or strong as it appears

examples.x: Despite their vows of loyalty, their partnership turned out to be a rope of sand, as they quickly drifted apart.
The fragile peace agreement between the two warring nations resembled a rope of sand, shattered by the slightest provocation.
The project's success was hanging by a thread, like a rope of sand, with inadequate planning and resources.
Their trust was like a rope of sand, unable to withstand the strains and pressures of their strained relationship.
In a world where alliances can be as fragile as a rope of sand, it's essential to establish solid foundations for cooperation.

senses.definition: suspected or disapproved of due to previous deeds

examples.x: After the financial scandal, the company's CEO was under a cloud of suspicion, and his reputation was tarnished.
The teacher had been under a cloud of suspicion since the cheating incident in the classroom, which led to her suspension.

word_list.word: how long is a piece of string
word_list.pronunciation: hˌaʊ lˈɑːŋ ɪz ɐ pˈiːs ʌv stɹˈɪŋ

senses.definition: used to say that it is impossible to answer a question concerning the size, length, or amount of something

examples.x: When asked how long it will take to complete a complex project, the project manager replied, "How long is a piece of string?"
When inquired about the number of possible variations for the design, the architect humorously asked, "How long is a piece of string?"

senses.definition: used to say that the amount mentioned might be a little more or less than the exact amount

examples.x: The recipe calls for 2 cups of sugar, give or take a tablespoon.
The meeting is scheduled for 3:00 PM, give or take a few minutes for potential delays.
The car's fuel efficiency is about 30 miles per gallon, give or take a mile or two depending on driving conditions.
The package should arrive in 5 business days, give or take a day for any unexpected delays.
She estimated the project would cost around $10,000, give or take a few hundred dollars depending on material prices.

senses.definition: used to describe a situation where no clear advantage or resolution has emerged, leaving the final result uncertain

examples.x: After hours of negotiation, the talks are still neck and neck, with no clear resolution in sight.
The debate ended up being neck and neck, as neither side could definitively prove its point.
The courtroom drama left the jury members in deliberation, and the case remained neck and neck until a verdict was reached.

senses.definition: used to describe a person or thing that is hard to describe or classify

examples.x: Medical interns are neither fish nor fowl.
The new hybrid car model is neither fish nor fowl; it doesn't strictly belong to the electric or gasoline categories.
Her unique style of art is neither fish nor fowl in the art world, making it challenging to define or categorize.
The product's marketing campaign was a bit of both traditional and modern strategies, making it neither fish nor fowl.
The building's architectural design was neither fish nor fowl, blending several different styles to create something distinctive yet challenging to categorize.

senses.definition: a very little or no chance of success
(of a person) to be in a state of uncertainty or confusion

examples.x: Meanwhile, her New York career hangs in the wind as it seems she's about to be passed over for a huge promotion.
The employees felt like they were twisting in the wind as they awaited news of impending layoffs.
With no clear instructions from the management, the project was left to twist in the wind.
After the sudden resignation of the CEO, the company's future was left twisting in the wind.
